greater-glasgow-life-sciences.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Life Sciences: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, four subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-life-sciences.caption: Anchored by the University of Glasgow (31%) and Strathclyde (20%), and reinforced by Glasgow Caledonian University, NHS Greater Glasgow & Clyde and the IBioIC industrial-biotech innovation centre, the cluster attracts £1.07bn of UKRI funding across 4,329 grants.

greater-glasgow-space.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Space: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-space.caption: Largely a University of Strathclyde story — 86% of the region's £229m in UKRI space funding — supported by Glasgow-built satellite firms including Clyde Space, Craft Prospect and Wideblue, within one of Europe's leading small-satellite manufacturing hubs.

greater-glasgow-net-zero.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Net Zero: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-net-zero.caption: Glasgow and Strathclyde lead the academic base of the cluster (29% and 20% respectively), with contributions from Nitech Solutions, ScottishPower and Loganair supporting £1.07bn of UKRI-funded work in decarbonisation, power systems and clean aviation.

greater-glasgow-digital-creative.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Digital Creative: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-digital-creative.caption: The region's largest UKRI research cluster — £1.12bn across 4,344 grants — combines University of Glasgow (37%) and Strathclyde (27%) strengths in computing and AI with photonics and compound-semiconductor specialists including M Squared Lasers and CST Global.

greater-glasgow-advanced-manufacturing.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Advanced Manufacturing: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-advanced-manufacturing.caption: Anchored by Strathclyde (38%) — with NMIS and AFRC underpinning much of that activity — the cluster connects Kelvin Nanotechnology, Weir Group, Nitech and Spirit AeroSystems into the Clyde's advanced manufacturing and aerostructures supply chain, representing £459m in UKRI funding.

greater-glasgow-fintech.alt: Diagnostic flow for Greater Glasgow Fintech: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow-fintech.caption: While branded as fintech, the cluster is primarily research-led: the Universities of Glasgow (48%) and Strathclyde (36%) account for most UKRI funding, centred on data science, financial modelling, cyber security and risk analytics.

west-of-england-aerospace.alt: Diagnostic flow for West of England Aerospace: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
west-of-england-aerospace.caption: The region's largest UKRI cluster — £1.38bn across 952 grants — combines University of Bristol research strength (22%) with the Filton aerospace manufacturing base centred on Airbus, Renishaw and the National Composites Centre.

west-of-england-clean-tech.alt: Diagnostic flow for West of England Clean Tech: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
west-of-england-clean-tech.caption: University of Bristol (29%) and the Environment Agency (18%) lead a low-carbon innovation cluster that also includes Progressive Energy, ZeroAvia and Cavendish Nuclear, together representing £1.10bn across 749 UKRI grants.

west-of-england-creative.alt: Diagnostic flow for West of England Creative: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
west-of-england-creative.caption: A comparatively small £96m cluster combining Bristol (26%), Creative UK, UWE, TRL Technology, Vero UK and Rough Trade Records — representing the region's creative-industries layer alongside its larger engineering and aerospace strengths.

west-of-england-digital.alt: Diagnostic flow for West of England Digital: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
west-of-england-digital.caption: University of Bristol (33%) anchors a digital and cyber-oriented cluster shaped by Airbus, Renishaw and the wider cyber-security ecosystem linked to Cheltenham, totalling £704m across 454 UKRI grants.

west-of-england-fin-tech.alt: Diagnostic flow for West of England Fintech: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
west-of-england-fin-tech.caption: Bristol (39%) and FinTech West (19%) anchor a modest £67m cluster that is more research-and-network-led than driven by large-scale commercial fintech firms.

west-of-england-life-sciences.alt: Diagnostic flow for West of England Life Sciences: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
west-of-england-life-sciences.caption: The University of Bristol leads 58% of the region's £970m life sciences cluster, with additional activity across Renishaw, Gloucestershire Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust and UWE — making this one of the most Bristol-centric clusters in the wider West of England ecosystem.

west-of-england-space.alt: Diagnostic flow for West of England Space: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
west-of-england-space.caption: A small but specialised £37m cluster dominated by KETS Quantum Security (60%) and the University of Bristol (27%), reflecting regional strengths in quantum security and positioning technologies.

hull-east-yorkshire-advanced-manufacturi.alt: Diagnostic flow for Hull & East Yorkshire Advanced Manufacturing: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
hull-east-yorkshire-advanced-manufacturi.caption: An unusually industry-heavy cluster: Croda Europe alone leads 35% (£231m), with York at 28% and contributions from Beta Technology, the Food Standards Agency and Reckitt Benckiser — making this the region's most industrially anchored UKRI cluster.

hull-east-yorkshire-clean-energy.alt: Diagnostic flow for Hull & East Yorkshire Clean Energy: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
hull-east-yorkshire-clean-energy.caption: York (40%) and Hull (12%) lead the academic base of a clean-energy cluster that also includes Drax Power and British Steel, representing £486m of UKRI funding linked to Humber decarbonisation.

hull-east-yorkshire-digital.alt: Diagnostic flow for Hull & East Yorkshire Digital: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
hull-east-yorkshire-digital.caption: Strongly York-led at 60%, the cluster also includes the University of Hull, Endeavour Health Charitable Trust and Network Communication Systems, totalling £253m across 248 UKRI grants.

hull-east-yorkshire-health-tech.alt: Diagnostic flow for Hull & East Yorkshire Health Tech: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, subclusters, and outcome distribution.
hull-east-yorkshire-health-tech.caption: University of York dominates at 59%, with the University of Hull (8%) and a wider network of translational firms — including Neotherix, AgriFood X, mTuitive and Intelsius — contributing across 821 grants.

greater-glasgow.alt: Diagnostic flow for the Greater Glasgow innovation ecosystem: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, six clusters, and outcome distribution.
greater-glasgow.caption: Two anchor universities — Glasgow and Strathclyde — six interlocking research clusters, and a dense Clyde-corridor network of NHS hospitals, innovation centres and advanced supply-chain firms together account for £4.56bn of UKRI funding across 5,718 grants.

west-of-england.alt: Diagnostic flow for the West of England innovation ecosystem: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, clusters, and outcome distribution.
west-of-england.caption: University of Bristol accounts for £1.47bn — 34% of the £4.36bn drawn across the region's seven UKRI-active clusters — with Renishaw, Airbus, the National Composites Centre and the Filton clean-tech and aerospace corridor reinforcing the wider innovation ecosystem.

hull-east-yorkshire.alt: Diagnostic flow for the Hull & East Yorkshire innovation ecosystem: UKRI funders, anchor institutions, clusters, and outcome distribution.
hull-east-yorkshire.caption: University of York is the dominant anchor — £884m, 45% of the £1.98bn its four active clusters draw between them — with Croda Europe, Drax and British Steel contributing much of the industrial base.

cta.framing: Same data examined through five diagnostic lenses — Pipeline, Leverage, Triple Helix, Throughput, Collaboration. The interactive diagnostic is currently in private preview.

source_line: Sources: UKRI Gateway to Research (grants, outcomes); OpenAlex (publications); Companies House (spin-out lifecycle); Crunchbase (disclosed capital). Snapshot May 2026.
